Hey, Phil here with MVL 2.0. Just want to give you a real brief overview of lead scoring and where you can find some of these elements in your active campaign account. So to be clear, this concept of lead scoring is a very deep conversation. It's something we go really far down the rabbit hole with in AI. We developed this system of what we call AI rules. We provide you with 15 out-of-the-box automations to get you started. We talk about how you're going to tailor this to what is true in your business, and we build this all out together. So you really have this fine tune machine working for you in the background of your business. But where I want to start here in MVL 2.0, it's just really familiarizing you with where these elements live within active campaign. So you can start to just play with this yourself and see a little bit what's possible. So what I'm going to show you here, if you remember in one of the previous videos, we discussed some of those key lead scoring definitions we want to have. We want to have email opens, email clicks, key web page visits, webinar registrations, and email replies. Those are the minimum and there's many, many more that you will develop over time in your business. But I'm going to just show you one example of this. So I'm going to go to my AI rules here, as I mentioned, we have these 15, and I'm going to show you just one. So this is scoring for cart page views. So this is an example of a key page visit. So what you can see here is any automation and active campaign has what's called a trigger, and you can create multiple triggers. So some of those definitions that I just mentioned, they open an email, they click the link, they visit a web page, those are all examples of triggers. So you set that based on the behavior having been taken by your prospects, your pre-customers. So in this example, we have a contact visiting this kind of fictitious page here, automatedintimacy.com forward slash cart. And so we are in this case running it multiple times. So this is saying when a contact visits that page, we are going to increment their AI rule. So this is this AI rule algorithm that we've designed. We're going to add 20 points to that. And so this is just a action that you can take. It's under contacts. You'll see that there's adjust a contact score. And once you've created your kind of scoring algorithm, you will be able to select it there and adjust that in increments up and down, depending on what you are looking to kind of weight in your system. So the last aspect I want to quickly show you here is, if you go in your active campaign account to forward slash score, this is where you can create this score. So you can see where I have AI rules. So this actually becomes a custom field. It's a custom contact score that I can see on an individual contact record that has been incremented up or down based on the various behaviors that people are taking. So if I go to my contacts here and just look an example, here's how that looks. So you can see that AI rules, this particular contact has a score of 55. And that is all happening based on these different automations that are running in the background, screening and analyzing the key behaviors and actions that our pre-customers and customers are taking. So that's it for this video. Again, this is just kind of the jumping off point for a much bigger conversation. But I would definitely encourage you to just check out some of active campaigns, resources around lead scoring, and just play with some of the ideas that have shown you here today and see if you can start to add some of these aspects into your business. Much love. Take care.
